name: South Park Commons India
description: Pre-ideation to early-stage founder support from South Park Commons' $40M India Fund. Partnership with Binny Bansal (Flipkart co-founder). Focus on founders at the -1 to 0 phase. Global network with founders from Stanford, MIT, and industry leaders.

name: HAX (SOSV)
description: Most comprehensive hardware development program. 6-month residency with access to mechanical, chemical, and electronics experts. Global network of 440+ portfolio companies.

name: Y Combinator
description: Leading startup accelerator. Standard deal: $125K for 7% equity + $375K MFN SAFE. Unparalleled Demo Day visibility.

name: Techstars
description: Decentralized mentor-driven model with 50+ programs worldwide. 'Mentor Madness' connects founders with 100+ mentors in first month.

name: Brinc India
description: 4-month program combining online education with practical manufacturing support. Tracks for AgriTech, HealthTech, FoodTech.

name: Creative Destruction Lab (CDL)
description: Non-dilutive mentorship model. Objective-setting process coached by world-renowned scientists. No equity taken, no fees charged.

name: NIDHI-PRAYAS (India)
description: Department of Science & Technology (DST) program. Non-repayable grant for product development over 18 months. Bridges 'valley of death' between POC and prototype.

name: BIRAC BIG (India)
description: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council grant. For biotech, med-tech, and diagnostic hardware. Supports innovation in healthcare technologies.

name: TIDE 2.0 (India)
description: Ministry of Electronics & IT program. Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs. Supports ICT-enabled hardware in agritech, health-tech.

name: USISTEF (India–USA)
description: US–India Science & Technology Endowment Fund. Bilateral R&D for commercialization. Focus on Critical & Emerging Technology (iCET), quantum, AI.

name: IGSTC 2+2 Call (India–Germany)
description: Indo-German Science & Technology Centre. Supports market-ready solutions in mHealth, telemedicine, water resource management.

name: NLnet / NGI0 (Europe)
description: European Commission funding for open-source hardware. Supports decentralized communications, secure firmware, accessibility hardware. All outputs must be FOSS.

name: Mozilla Technology Fund
description: Democracy × AI cohort. For hardware that strengthens civic knowledge or institutional transparency. Requires working prototype in use.

name: SPARK / SINE (IIT Bombay)
description: Scale-up grant for technology startups from IIT Bombay's SINE incubator. Supports early-stage tech ventures to scale.

name: Elemental Excelerator
description: Non-dilutive climate tech funding. Supports hardware for clean energy, climate adaptation, sustainable materials.

name: Hackaday Prize
description: Annual open hardware competition for social impact projects. Multiple prize categories and stages.

name: Antler India
description: 3-week residency in Bangalore with access to $1M in AI perks. Strong network of corporate partners and investors.

name: Iterate.vc
description: Focused on Southeast Asia and India. Remote program for hardware and deep-tech startups.

name: All In Capital
description: All In Capital is an India-focused early-stage fund investing in startups across deep tech, hardware, and consumer sectors. Backs founders from pre-seed through Series A. Part of the broader Indian startup ecosystem with ties to Bengaluru and Delhi NCR founder communities.

name: Speciale Invest
description: Speciale Invest is a Bengaluru-based deep-tech focused VC backing semiconductor, space, defence, and advanced manufacturing startups. One of few Indian VCs with explicit hardware manufacturing and deep tech thesis.

name: IdeaSpring Capital
description: Bengaluru-based seed fund focused on hardware, robotics, IoT, and connected devices. One of India's most active hardware-specific investors. Portfolio includes robotics, medical devices, and industrial IoT companies.

name: GUSEC (Gujarat Uni Startup & Entrepreneurship Council)
eligibility: Gujarat / India startups and student founders
description: GUSEC is one of India's top ranked university incubators based in Ahmedabad. Provides grant funding, infrastructure, mentorship, and government scheme access (SSIP, NIDHI-PRAYAS) for hardware and tech startups. Particularly active for IoT and manufacturing.

name: T-Hub — Hyderabad
eligibility: India startups, global with India ops
description: T-Hub is India's largest startup incubator based in Hyderabad. Government-backed but operates like a private accelerator. Has dedicated hardware and deep-tech programmes. Provides grants, lab access, corporate partnerships, and regulatory support.

name: SID / SINE — IIT Bombay
eligibility: IIT Bombay faculty, students, alumni; India startups
description: Society for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (SINE) at IIT Bombay incubates deep-tech startups with lab access, prototype funding, and connection to IIT Bombay research. Strong in hardware, medical devices, energy, and manufacturing.

name: NASSCOM CoE — IoT & AI
description: NASSCOM Centre of Excellence for IoT and AI provides hardware startups with lab access, corporate pilot opportunities, grants, and mentorship. Has facilities in Bengaluru and Gurugram. Well-connected to enterprise hardware buyers.
